When VIX is above 24, cut normal position size by 30-50 percent and reduce overnight holds unless the trade has a hard catalyst.
Treat oil spikes as an index-level risk factor: if WTI is above 95 and rising intraday, lower long beta and avoid chasing growth breakouts.
Do not trade on rate-cut hope when policy is explicitly on hold; as of March 18, 2026 the Fed kept 3.50-3.75 percent and guidance stayed data dependent.
Require confirmation from rates for long equity breakouts: if the 10-year yield is rising fast (for example 4.25 to 4.38 in one session), assume valuation pressure on high-duration names.
In headline-driven markets, take partial profits sooner (first scale-out at 1R) because reversals happen faster than in low-volatility trends.
Use relative-strength filters by sector before stock selection: 2026 leadership has favored energy, industrials, and consumer defensives over mega-cap growth.
Avoid averaging down in semiconductors or AI beta during legal/geopolitical shock headlines; wait for a daily close back above prior-day high first.
If three out of four S&P names close red, treat dip-buy attempts as tactical mean reversion only, not swing trend entries.
Run pairs when possible in risk-off weeks: long leaders (energy/defensives) against weak index beta to control market-direction risk.
When VIX has recently spiked above 28-30, expect more failed breakouts for several sessions and demand higher volume confirmation before entry.
Do not add risk in the last hour of triple-witching sessions; execution noise and hedging flows can invalidate otherwise clean setups.
Keep a hard daily loss cap (for example 1.5R) in elevated-volatility regimes to prevent one headline cycle from causing weekly damage.
If Fed-cut pricing is being removed quickly, favor cash-flow and pricing-power names over long-duration narratives.
Respect geopolitical weekend gap risk: cut gross exposure Friday if key conflict headlines are unresolved.
Use VWAP and prior-day levels as primary intraday anchors in choppy tape; ignore low-quality signals in the middle of the range.
Track regime state explicitly in every trade note (risk_on/risk_off/neutral); strategy performance drifts when regime tags are missing.
Do not confuse short-covering rallies with regime change; require both S&P reclaiming short-term trend and VIX cooling before size-up.
In high-volatility markets, execution quality matters more than prediction quality; use limit entries and preplanned exits.
If commodity volatility risk premium is widening, assume cross-asset stress can spill into equities with little warning and reduce leverage.
Keep 30-40 percent deployable cash in risk-off conditions so forced selling is never required during event spikes.
